steps.title: Recognize the parabola opens upward with minimum at its vertex
steps.content: The function $f(x) = 4x^2 - 50x + 126$ has leading coefficient $a = 4 \gt 0$. Since the parabola opens upward, its minimum value occurs at the vertex. We need the x-coordinate of this vertex.

steps.title: Calculate the x-coordinate of the vertex
steps.content: With coefficients $a = 4$ and $b = -50$: $$x = -\frac{(-50)}{2(4)} = \frac{50}{8} = \frac{25}{4}$$ The minimum occurs at $x = \frac{25}{4}$.
